mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic acceleration that outpaces supply constraints, boosting aggregate demand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge for more than two consecutive months without SPX reacting as predicted.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, sustained increases in the consumer price index force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% month-over-month for three consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral during periods where CPIAUCSL meets the three-month rising threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly reflects expanding corporate profit potential, mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day forward return median remains below zero during quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after adjusting for seasonal earnings cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals accelerating labor demand, which boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances following a >0.3pp UNRATE drop, or the signal fails to predict returns after controlling for PAYEMS revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while payroll employment expands rapidly, the combination of accommodative liquidity and strengthening fundamentals creates a risk-on environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS/PAYEMS signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when added to a baseline macro factor model.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained periods, a sustained drop in the industrial production index combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates demand-pull inflation that erodes corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% month-over-month while RSAFS rises > 2.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO declines and RSAFS rises simultaneously, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 5-year rolling window.
